description:
Timberborn is a city-building survival game where you manage a colony of
beavers through droughts and badtides. Building blueprints that normally
cost Science Points are shuffled into the multiworld — you must survive on
whatever tech arrives while sending checks to unlock buildings for others.
Supports Folktails and Iron Teeth factions.

v0.0.1.description:
## Pre-Alpha Playtest

> **WARNING: This release is NOT in a fully playable state.** It is provided strictly for testing and development purposes. **DO NOT add this to multiworld games** unless every player in the session knows you are testing an incomplete integration and the host has explicitly agreed to it. This can and will cause issues in your multiworld.

> This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied. In no event shall the authors be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability arising from the use of this software. Use at your own risk.

This is an **early playtest build** of the Archipelago Multiworld integration for Timberborn. Expect bugs, missing features, and breaking changes between versions. Released to enable community playtesting and feedback.

**The only goal that has been properly tested is the Population goal.** Other goals may not work correctly or at all.

Feedback and bug reports are welcome in the [Issues tab](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/issues).

### What's included

**Client Mod (Archipelago.zip)**
- Archipelago client mod for Timberborn (Folktails + Iron Teeth factions)
- Randomized building unlocks via AP shop with tiered progression
- Traps, filler items, boosts, scouts, and skip tickets
- Save/load persistence of AP state
- Weather trap scheduling with notice period

**APWorld (timberborn.apworld)**
- Timberborn world definition for Archipelago
- Tiered shop layout with escalating Science costs
- Branching-path progression logic with soft-lock prevention
- Population, well-being, survival, and wonder goal categories
- Configurable YAML options (faction, goals, difficulty, trap mode, etc.)

### Installation
1. Extract `Archipelago.zip` into your Timberborn mods folder (`Documents/Timberborn/Mods/`)
2. Place `timberborn.apworld` in your Archipelago `custom_worlds/` folder
3. Requires [**Archipelago 0.6.7**](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7-rc1)

### Known limitations
- Balance is still being tuned
- May not survive Timberborn game updates without mod changes
v0.0.1.title: v0.0.1 - Pre-Alpha Playtest

v0.0.2.description:
## Pre-Alpha Playtest

> **WARNING: This release is NOT in a fully playable state.** It is provided strictly for testing and development purposes. **DO NOT add this to multiworld games** unless every player in the session knows you are testing an incomplete integration and the host has explicitly agreed to it. This can and will cause issues in your multiworld.

> This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied. In no event shall the authors be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability arising from the use of this software. Use at your own risk.

This is an **early playtest build** of the Archipelago Multiworld integration for Timberborn. Expect bugs, missing features, and breaking changes between versions. Released to enable community playtesting and feedback.

**The only goal that has been properly tested is the Population goal.** Other goals may not work correctly or at all.

Feedback and bug reports are welcome in the [Issues tab](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/issues).

### Changes since v0.0.1

**Bug fixes (C# mod):**
- Drought/badtide traps now visually start in-game (was silently failing)
- Survival milestones no longer fire immediately on connect (baseline snapshot)
- Fixed NeedManager resolution for hunger/thirst traps (was using non-existent API)
- Fixed BonusManager/NeedManager component lookup (Timberborn's custom component system)
- Fixed compile error: use GameCycleService.CycleDay instead of WeatherService.CycleDay

**Bug fixes (APWorld):**
- Skip and trap item counts now scale dynamically to fit available locations
- Previously 5 items were silently dropped when the pool exceeded location count

### Known issues
- Boost items may still not apply (BonusManager lookup needs runtime verification)
- Hunger/thirst traps need runtime verification of need ID strings
- Population milestones trigger instantly on maps with 14+ starting beavers (free check)
- Weather trap visual confirmation still needs testing
- Balance is still being tuned

### Installation
1. Extract `Archipelago.zip` into your Timberborn mods folder (`Documents/Timberborn/Mods/`)
2. Place `timberborn.apworld` in your Archipelago `custom_worlds/` folder
3. Requires [**Archipelago 0.6.7**](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7-rc1)
v0.0.2.title: v0.0.2 - Pre-Alpha Playtest

v0.0.3.description:
## Pre-Alpha Playtest

> **WARNING: This release is NOT in a fully playable state.** It is provided strictly for testing and development purposes. **DO NOT add this to multiworld games** unless every player in the session knows you are testing an incomplete integration and the host has explicitly agreed to it. This can and will cause issues in your multiworld.

> This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied. In no event shall the authors be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability arising from the use of this software. Use at your own risk.

v0.0.3 is a focused stability and correctness pass - most of the day-one runtime bugs in v0.0.2 are now fixed and runtime-verified. Feedback welcome in the [Issues tab](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/issues).

### Critical bug fixes (C# mod)

- **Crash on first boost purchase fixed.** v0.0.2 threw \`ArgumentException: Invalid generic arguments\` when applying a boost, trap-on-entity, or filler because \`EntityComponentRegistry.GetEnabled<T>\` has a \`where T : BaseComponent, IRegisteredComponent\` constraint that \`MakeGenericMethod\` enforces at runtime. Now uses \`EntityRegistry.Entities\` + \`BaseComponent.GetComponent<T>()\` per entity (unconstrained, safe).
- **Filler goods injection fixed.** \`Inventory\` extends \`BaseComponent\`, not \`UnityEngine.Object\`, so \`FindObjectsByType\` always returned empty. Now finds stockpiles correctly and uses \`Inventory.Gives()\` to prefer stockpiles over beaver-carry inventories.
- **Boost re-apply on save/reload fixed.** Bonuses were silently lost after reload because \`Load()\` ran before entities were instantiated. Moved to \`PostLoad()\` where entities actually exist. Runtime-verified: \`MovementSpeed: 1.25\` persists across reload on 200+ entity saves.
- **Hazardous Weather trap now actually triggers hazardous weather.** v0.0.2 updated history counters (enough for milestone firing) but didn't transition the visual weather state. Now shortens the current temperate cycle via \`TemperateWeatherDurationService\` backing field; the game's own cycle-end handler transitions through the proper event chain with the native "Drought/Badtide approaching" countdown UI.

### New features

- **\`Trap: Hazardous Weather\` replaces \`Trap: Early Drought\` + \`Trap: Badwater Leak\`.** Single consolidated trap item; the game's own \`HazardousWeatherRandomizer\` picks drought or badtide based on map conditions. Honest semantics on maps without badwater.
- **\`Extra Early Survival\` YAML option** (default on) - forces one random housing/food/wellbeing building per category into sphere 1 alongside the existing essentials. 50-seed validation shows +60pp food and +46pp wellbeing availability on Folktails. Requires \`force_early_items\` and milestones enabled. Toggle off for leaner seeds.
- **Population milestone "Reach 10 Beavers" → "Reach 15 Beavers".** 10 was below starting population on normal (14), so it auto-fired on connect. 15 requires actual growth.
- **\`BonusId\` validation at load** - the mod now diffs the hardcoded \`BoostMapping\` BonusIds against the live \`BonusTypeSpecService\` on startup and warns if any are unknown. Would have caught all 4 v0.0.1 boost string bugs at load.
- **Reflection dry-runs at load** - the mod exercises every reflection path on startup so runtime-constraint failures surface in \`Player.log\` at connect instead of mid-gameplay.
- **\`ConnectionBlocked\` resets on save load** - a faction mismatch used to permanently block reconnects until you fully restarted Timberborn. Now clears on every save load.
- **\`IPostLoadableSingleton\` diagnostic snapshot** logs entity / BonusManager / Inventory / NeedManager / beaver / bot counts at load so you can sanity-check scale.

### Known issues (open follow-ups)

- **Hungry / Thirsty Beavers traps silent no-op.** \`NeedManager\` reflection paths (\`<AllNeeds>k__BackingField\`, \`Need.NeedId\`) don't resolve against the current \`Timberborn.NeedSystem.dll\`. Pending DLL byte-regex diagnosis (pythonnet can't load NeedSystem due to its \`ImmutableArray\` dependency).
- **T3 progression items can land at T1 shop slots.** \`Rules.py\` slot access rules don't constrain item placement by item tier, so \`Smelter\` (T3) can appear at \`Shop: C-01\` (T1). Construction costs still gate actual use, but the sequence feels loose. APWorld-side follow-up.
- **Boosts apply to bots in addition to beavers.** \`BonusManager\` is attached to both. Some bonuses are semantically nonsensical on bots (\`LifeExpectancy\`, \`GrowthSpeed\`); bonuses the engine doesn't apply to bots just no-op internally.
- **"Unknown location: Victory: Population" warning** on goal completion. Cosmetic - goal still fires correctly via \`SendGoalCompleted\` StatusUpdate path.

### Installation

1. Extract \`Archipelago.zip\` into your Timberborn mods folder (\`Documents/Timberborn/Mods/\`)
2. Place \`timberborn.apworld\` in your Archipelago \`custom_worlds/\` folder
3. Requires [**Archipelago 0.6.7**](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7-rc1)

### Full changelog

- \`ef9d997\` crash fix: EntityRegistry + BaseComponent.GetComponent<T>
- \`a3595a0\` ReadOnlyList<T> null-check fix; load-time reflection dry-runs
- \`0f68cba\` PostLoad boost re-apply timing + diagnostic
- \`ce5b764\` ConnectionBlocked resets on save load
- \`a988215\` milestone comment sync with APWorld rename
- \`6a14df5\` filler Inventory BaseComponent fix
- \`8e05c87\` DiscoverBonusIds validator fleshed out
- \`14d87f9\` Hazardous Weather trap redesign
- \`2c38099\` TemperateWeatherDuration backing-field reflection
- \`d58b343\` explicit _trapScheduledAwaitingTransition flag
- \`2253b9d\` simplified Hazardous Weather to temperate-shortening only
- \`9b371e9\` manifest bump to 0.0.3.0 + IT faction description
v0.0.3.title: v0.0.3 - Pre-Alpha Playtest

v0.0.4.description:
## Pre-Alpha Playtest

> **WARNING: This release is NOT in a fully playable state.** It is provided strictly for testing and development purposes. **DO NOT add this to multiworld games** unless every player in the session knows you are testing an incomplete integration and the host has explicitly agreed to it. This can and will cause issues in your multiworld.

> This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied. In no event shall the authors be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability arising from the use of this software. Use at your own risk.

v0.0.4 is a maintenance release over [v0.0.3](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/releases/tag/v0.0.3). Two fixes beyond v0.0.3's feature set:

### Fixes in v0.0.4

- **Fill-failure in `_force_random_early_survival` on wonder-only milestone configs.** Fuzz caught a 3.3% generation failure rate when `extra_early_survival=true` and none of population / well-being / survival milestones were enabled. Example: with only `include_wonder_milestone: true`, the APWorld tried to force 4 core items + 3 survival extras into 4 sphere-1 shop slots (Wonder is T5, not sphere-1 reachable). The `_force_random_early_survival` guard now requires population milestones specifically - they contribute 3 T1 locations (First Beaver Born, First Grown Up, Reach 15 Beavers) that give the fill algorithm enough sphere-1 budget for the extras. Without population milestones, the extras are silently skipped. Fuzz rerun after the fix: 0 failures in 5000 default-preset seeds.
- **Metadata alignment.** `ArchipelagoWorld/worlds/timberborn/archipelago.json` now correctly reports `"world_version": "0.0.4"`. v0.0.3's tag had this file stale at `0.0.2`, which made the generated YAML template header advertise the wrong required version.

### Everything shipped in v0.0.3 is also in v0.0.4

For the full v0.0.3 feature/fix list (crash fix, PostLoad boost re-apply, ConnectionBlocked reset, Hazardous Weather trap redesign, DiscoverBonusIds validator, reflection dry-runs, filler goods injection fix, ExtraEarlySurvival option, population milestone threshold bump), see the [v0.0.3 release notes](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/releases/tag/v0.0.3).

### Known issues (unchanged from v0.0.3)

- **Hungry / Thirsty Beavers traps silent no-op.** `NeedManager` reflection paths (`<AllNeeds>k__BackingField`, `Need.NeedId`) don't resolve against the current `Timberborn.NeedSystem.dll`. Pending DLL byte-regex diagnosis.
- **T3 progression items can land at T1 shop slots.** Fill algorithm places items based on slot access rules but doesn't tier-gate by item requirements. Smelter (T3) can appear at Shop: C-01 (T1). Construction costs still gate actual use.
- **Boosts apply to bots in addition to beavers.** BonusManager attaches to both.
- **"Unknown location: Victory: Population" warning** on goal completion. Cosmetic, goal still fires correctly.

### Installation

1. Extract `Archipelago.zip` into your Timberborn mods folder (`Documents/Timberborn/Mods/`)
2. Place `timberborn.apworld` in your Archipelago `custom_worlds/` folder
3. Requires [**Archipelago 0.6.7**](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7)

### New: YAML template

`Timberborn.yaml` attached - a verbose weighted YAML template with every option documented. Drop into your Archipelago `Players/` folder as a starting point.

### Changelog since v0.0.3

Mod:
- `aeaca86` Replace em dash with hyphen in manifest description
- `5177531` Bump manifest to v0.0.4.0

APWorld:
- `5c1a4816` Bump archipelago.json world_version to 0.0.3 (mid-v0.0.3 hotfix)
- `b78364bd` Bump archipelago.json world_version to 0.0.4
- `014ca3a5` Guard `_force_random_early_survival` on population milestones (fuzz fix)
v0.0.4.title: v0.0.4 - Pre-Alpha Playtest

v0.0.5.description:
## Pre-Alpha Playtest

> Pre-alpha. Most features have been verified end-to-end in playtesting but a few item types remain untested at runtime - see "Known issues" below. If you join a multiworld with this release, mention to your group that you're running a pre-alpha; otherwise it should behave well in single-player and small multi-player setups.
>
> Provided as-is, no warranty. The authors are not liable for any issues arising from use.

v0.0.5 is a Hazardous Weather trap mechanism overhaul over [v0.0.4](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/releases/tag/v0.0.4). The visible behavior change: a Hazardous Weather trap now arrives with the game's native 3-day "Drought/Badtide approaching" countdown notification, hazardous runs its full intended duration, and the cycle ends cleanly afterwards. v0.0.4's extended-badtide bug (~17-day hazardous instead of 7) is fixed.

### Why the redesign

v0.0.3 / v0.0.4 used temperate-shortening (reflection-write `<TemperateWeatherDuration>k__BackingField` to `currentDay + 3`) and let the game's natural cycle transition fire hazardous. UX was correct (3-day countdown) but the trap-induced badtide ran far longer than intended on long-running saves like Crator. Drought testing on v0.0.4 surfaced the symptom as "water spawning broken in the following temperate cycle." Cause was misdiagnosed as state-bleed across the cycle boundary.

The v0.0.5 first attempt was direct-trigger via `StartHazardousWeather()`. That fixed the duration math but produced wrong player UX - the hazardous fired instantly with no notification.

The v0.0.5 final design (this release) returns to temperate-shortening but also writes `GameCycleService._cycleDurationInDays` to match the new `TempDur + HazDur`. The discovery during Session J runtime verification: `_cycleDurationInDays` is a CACHE computed at cycle start from the registered `ICycleDuration` providers, and the game's `OnDaytimeStart` cycle-end check (`day > _cycleDurationInDays → StartNextCycle()`) reads the cached value, not the live providers. Mutating only `TemperateWeatherDuration` shortened the temperate phase but left the cycle running its original full length, so hazardous extended from the (correctly early) transition all the way to the original cycle boundary - typically ~17 days instead of HazDur=7. The water-spawn regression observed in v0.0.4 was almost certainly the prolonged badtide depleting water-source state past quick recovery, not a state-bleed across cycles. Writing both backing fields together fixes the duration AND the regression at its root.

### Trap behavior in v0.0.5

- Trap arrives during temperate → game's native "Drought/Badtide approaching" notification appears with 3-day countdown widget in the top-right
- After 3 in-game days, hazardous fires via the game's normal `WeatherService` event chain; drought-vs-badtide is whatever the game's own `HazardousWeatherRandomizer` had pre-rolled for the cycle
- Hazardous runs its full intended duration (HazDur, randomized per cycle)
- Cycle ends naturally at `newTempDur + HazDur` days; next cycle starts in a clean state via `GameCycleService.StartNextCycle()`, which re-randomizes `TemperateWeatherDuration` and recomputes `_cycleDurationInDays` from fresh providers
- During an active hazardous (or while another trap is still queued), additional traps queue with `trap_mode: queue` (default) or are discarded with `trap_mode: skip`

### Everything shipped in v0.0.4 is also in v0.0.5

For the full v0.0.4 fix list, see the [v0.0.4 release notes](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/releases/tag/v0.0.4). For the bigger v0.0.3 feature/fix list (crash fix, PostLoad boost re-apply, ConnectionBlocked reset, DiscoverBonusIds validator, reflection dry-runs, filler goods injection fix, ExtraEarlySurvival option, population milestone threshold bump), see the [v0.0.3 release notes](https://github.com/dowlle/timberborn-modding/releases/tag/v0.0.3).

### Known issues (unchanged from v0.0.4)

- **Hungry / Thirsty Beavers traps silent no-op.** `NeedManager` reflection paths (`<AllNeeds>k__BackingField`, `Need.NeedId`) don't resolve against the current `Timberborn.NeedSystem.dll`. The trap-fire summary log line (`[Archipelago/Trap] HUNGRY_BEAVERS fired: affected=0`) now appears even when the effect can't be applied, giving trap-delivery visibility we didn't have on v0.0.4.
- **T3 progression items can land at T1 shop slots.** Fill algorithm places items based on slot access rules but doesn't tier-gate by item requirements.
- **Boosts apply to bots in addition to beavers.** BonusManager attaches to both.
- **"Unknown location: Victory: Population" warning** on goal completion. Cosmetic, goal still fires correctly.
- **Water Storage goal silent-fail** on both factions (broken `GoodId` reflection path; deferred to v0.0.6+).
- **Bots goal unwinnable on Folktails** (no faction-gating in `_resolve_goals`; deferred to v0.0.6+).

### Installation

1. Extract `Archipelago.zip` into your Timberborn mods folder (`Documents/Timberborn/Mods/`)
2. Place `timberborn.apworld` in your Archipelago `custom_worlds/` folder
3. Requires [**Archipelago 0.6.7**](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/tag/0.6.7)

### YAML template

`Timberborn.yaml` attached - a verbose weighted YAML template with every option documented. Drop into your Archipelago `Players/` folder as a starting point.

### Changelog since v0.0.4

Mod:
- v0.0.5 Hazardous Weather trap redesign: queue + temperate-shortening + cycle-duration cache write + passive OnCycleStarted hook + EventBus injection
- Bump manifest to v0.0.5.0

APWorld:
- Bump archipelago.json world_version to 0.0.5 (no functional APWorld changes from v0.0.4)
v0.0.5.title: v0.0.5 - Pre-Alpha Playtest
